title: The Chamber Where Argument Becomes Understanding

features.name: the-three-lenses
features.desc-on-touch: Arranged like eyes, each one showing a different version of the same scene. Where they overlap, something new appears. You understand: sight is comparison.

features.name: refracted-argument
features.desc-on-touch: Light through the lenses bends an argument into three versions. The three don't agree, but they illuminate each other. Here, contradiction is a form of clarity.

features.name: eucalyptus-mirror
features.desc-on-touch: Its surface holds not just your reflection but versions of you from other angles. Each is equally true. None is complete without the others.

features.name: light-intersection
features.desc-on-touch: Three beams cross here. Where they meet, the light becomes something other than light. A new colour. An understanding that wasn't visible before.

features.name: the-resolution-arch
features.desc-on-touch: An opening formed by three perspectives, all leaning together. They don't agree. They have learned something better: how to hold shape despite disagreement.

inscription: the mirror learned it could not know itself alone; the lens learned that seeing requires refraction; together they understood understanding

file.name: Three_Versions_of_What_Happened_Here.txt
file.note: The archive recorded this location three times. The three accounts contradict perfectly. It has filed them under both truth and harmony.

exits.label: back toward the mirror where singular reflection still matters
toward chambers where comparison continues to multiply
into regions where three truths are learning to live as one
